response.justification: Over fifteen years of professional fullstack experience with a current role explicitly covering TypeScript, Node.js, React, AI-agent infrastructure, and MCP tooling maps almost directly onto the role's core responsibilities. Cloud and container exposure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker) is demonstrated through real delivery, and a sustained testing culture — including authoring test tooling — aligns with the team's Vitest/quality focus. The only meaningful gaps are the absence of Nest.js and Kotlin on the resume, both learnable given the depth shown in adjacent technologies.
strengths: Current role at re:cinq covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling — rare, direct match to the AI agent development requirement
Demonstrated fullstack delivery in the exact primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS) across multiple production roles
Deep testing culture evidenced by TDD practice across roles, authoring open-source test tooling, and running testing workshops
concerns: Nest.js not mentioned anywhere — may need ramp-up on the opinionated framework structure even with strong Node.js/TypeScript fundamentals
No Kotlin or JVM backend exposure despite it being a secondary stack requirement
Python absent from the profile, which is listed as a required language proficiency

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP tooling experience
Primary stack alignment (TS, Node, React, AWS, MongoDB)
